2025-10-17.thesis: White House weighs equity stakes in rare-earth/lithium miners
2025-10-17.exit_case: none — White House stake talk remains speculative ('weighs'/'may be eyeing'), no deal or stake announced yet
2025-10-17.assessment: Catalyst is fresh (entered same week) and specific: reports the administration is weighing direct equity stakes in rare-earth/lithium miners as a national-security/supply-chain response to China's rare-earth export curbs and the tit-for-tat tariff escalation. This is still early — coverage is limited to a handful of outlets (Fool.com), not yet mainstream-saturated, and no concrete government stake/deal has been announced. The driving condition (China curbs + US desire to onshore critical minerals) is intensifying, not resolving, given Trump's new 100% tariff threat this week. MP Materials remains the cleanest US-listed pure-play rare-earth miner that would be a natural target for such a stake.
2025-10-17.exit_advice: Hold MP; this is week one — catalyst is a live, unresolved policy deliberation (White House exploring equity stakes in rare-earth/lithium miners), amplified by escalating China rare-earth export curbs and Trump's new 100% tariff threat, which raises the odds of concrete US government action (stake, funding, offtake) on domestic critical-mineral supply chains. No stake has been taken, no bill signed, no deal closed — still speculative/pending per Fool.com's own framing ('Is the White House About to Invest'). Structural driver (critical minerals security policy) just getting fresh momentum from the China curb escalation, not fading.

The original JAXA satellite deal remains a discrete, already-public event with no reversal of the underlying smallsat-launch-demand/national-space-agency-contract driver, so this is not a hard exit or resolution. Silence decay has compounded for twenty-one straight weeks, pushing conviction to the absolute floor. No mainstream-saturation event nor arc-completion signal has fired either -- simply a dormant thesis. Rocket Lab earnings or a new contract announcement remain the flagged potential re-anchor points that could reset conviction upward; until then this position is inert and should be treated as a prime culling candidate.
2026-03-13.exit_advice: Hold as inert/low-priority; cull candidate given 21 weeks of silence, but no reversal or resolution to force a hard exit

2026-03-20.thesis: US strikes Iran's Kharg Island oil hub, Hormuz risk escalates
2026-03-20.exit_case: none - Kharg strike just occurred, Hormuz risk still escalating, no de-escalation or reopening yet
2026-03-20.assessment: US strike on Kharg Island is a datable, specific shock with oil prices resuming their advance and markets fixated on Strait of Hormuz risk. Tanker rates and oil benchmarks are the direct transmission channels. This is early-stage escalation, not yet priced to consensus - energy majors and oil-sensitive equities rallying but the acute chokepoint risk (actual Hormuz disruption) has not materialized or resolved. Basket of tanker plays (FRO, INSW, STNG) and oil proxy (USO) all remain live expressions of the same catalyst.
2026-03-20.exit_advice: hold - first week of acute escalation; watch for Hormuz closure/blockade or ceasefire as hard exit triggers

2026-03-27.thesis: US strikes Iran's Kharg Island oil hub, Hormuz risk escalates
2026-03-27.exit_case: Trump halted strikes on Iran; Gulf markets calm, ADNOC rerouted supply with no Hormuz disruption materializing
2026-03-27.assessment: The specific catalyst (US strikes on Kharg Island driving Hormuz escalation risk) has resolved: this week's news confirms Trump halted strikes on Iran, Gulf equity markets held steady, and ADNOC's chief noted expanded alternate routes avoided any supply disruption. The acute shock never escalated into an actual chokepoint closure and now the driving military action itself has stopped. This is a clean reversal of the driving condition, not mere silence - the strikes ended and markets have normalized. No fresh Hormuz/Iran escalation coverage this week; remaining news is unrelated nuclear-uranium sector price target commentary. Basket no longer expresses a live, forward catalyst.
2026-03-27.exit_advice: Exit tanker/oil basket - acute escalation has reversed with strikes halted and no chokepoint disruption realized; de-escalation confirmed by calm Gulf markets
